answer:
The recommended authentication method for Jira REST API integrations is OAuth 2.0 [1][2][3]. Atlassian Connect apps use JWT (JSON Web Tokens) [1][4][5], while personal access tokens (PATs) are supported for Data Center instances [2][3]. Basic authentication using an email address and API token is available but discouraged for production use due to lower security, and it should be reserved for simple scripts or bots [1][6][5]. For browser-based JavaScript, Jira uses standard cookie-based authentication [1][2][3]. 

To use basic authentication, encode your email and API token string as Base64 and include it in the header:

```bash
curl -D- \
   -X GET \
   -H "Authorization: Basic <base64_encoded_credentials>" \
   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
   "https://your-domain.atlassian.net/rest/api/2/issue/QA-31"
```

Reference [6] provides further details on generating tokens and constructing headers.
